info.name: Urban Management API
info.description: Smoke and integration test collection for the SmartCity Urban Management API. Updated to match the current apps/api contract.

request.description: Uses registerEmail/registerPhone from environment (defaults to nvhiep.aws@gmail.com/0901234567), then stores accessToken, refreshToken, currentUserId, and loginIdentity into the active Postman environment.
Requests OTP for citizen registration. Copy OTP from API log/webhook and set it to {{otpCode}}.
Logs in with {{loginIdentity}}/{{loginPassword}} and stores accessToken, refreshToken, and currentUserId into the active Postman environment.
Requests OTP for password-verified login.
Refreshes the current token pair and overwrites accessToken/refreshToken in the active Postman environment.

exec: const current = json?.success ? (json.data.find((session) => session.isCurrent) || json.data.find((session) => session.sessionId)) : null;
const history = json?.success ? (json.data.find((session) => Boolean(session.revokedAt) || Boolean(session.expiredAt)) || null) : null;

request.description: Dismisses a revoked or expired session from history. Run List Sessions first to auto-populate historySessionId.
Confirms forgot-password OTP and rotates account password.
Requests OTP to deactivate the currently authenticated account.
Confirms OTP and deactivates current account. Current access/refresh tokens are cleared.
Requests OTP to reactivate a deactivated account.
Confirms OTP, reactivates account, and stores new accessToken/refreshToken.
Requests OTP to permanently delete current account (requires active session).
Permanently deletes account after password + OTP check. Clears current tokens.

item.name: Auth Lifecycle - Guided Run
item.description: Run this folder in order to validate full lifecycle: ACTIVE -> DEACTIVATED -> ACTIVE -> DELETED. Prerequisites: loginIdentity/loginPassword point to an existing ACTIVE user, and otpCode is updated before each confirm step.

item.name: Flow - Friends & Discovery (Recommended Order)
item.description: Run requests in numeric order. Optional steps (03/06/09) depend on current relationship state and may return 4xx when not applicable.

item.name: Flow 10 - Discover Users (Chat Mode, After Cleanup)

request.description:
Upload avatar first with `uploadTarget=AVATAR`.
If `uploadEntityId` is blank, Postman will auto-use `currentUserId` when available.
Upload responses automatically save the returned key into `avatarKey`.

Use an older uploaded avatar from the avatar library as the current profile avatar.
Run `Uploads > List Avatar Uploads` first so Postman can populate `avatarLibraryKey`.
Removes only the current avatar reference from the user profile. Uploaded avatar files remain in the avatar library and can be reused later.

exec: const preferred = json.data.find((item) => item?.canSendFriendRequest === true) ?? json.data[0];
const nextUser = json?.success ? json.data.find((user) => user.id !== pm.environment.get('currentUserId')) : null;

body.raw:
{
  "groupName": "Postman Group {{$timestamp}}",
  "groupType": "TOPIC",
  "locationCode": "{{locationCode}}",
  "description": "Group created from Postman collection",
  "isOfficial": false
}

request.description:
Upload report media first with `uploadTarget=REPORT`.
Use `Upload Media` or `Presign Upload`; the response stores the key into `reportMediaKey` and `reportMediaKeysJson`.
This request sends `mediaKeys` directly from `reportMediaKeysJson`.

Use this after uploading new report media with `uploadTarget=REPORT`.
If `uploadEntityId` is blank, Postman auto-resolves it from `reportId`.
The request body is driven by `reportMediaKeysJson`.

Upload attachment first with `uploadTarget=MESSAGE`.
The upload response stores the returned key into `messageAttachmentKey`.
This request uses `attachmentKey={{messageAttachmentKey}}`.

Upload attachment first with `uploadTarget=MESSAGE`.
If `uploadEntityId` is blank, Postman auto-resolves it from `conversationId`.
The upload response stores the returned key into `messageAttachmentKey`.

Use after uploading a replacement attachment with `uploadTarget=MESSAGE`.
This request updates only the attachment field via `attachmentKey`.
The latest message attachment key is stored in `messageAttachmentKey`.

Primary end-user chat action for removing a message.
Use `EVERYONE` to keep a recalled placeholder in the thread for all participants.
Use `SELF` to hide the message only from the sender view.

Forward the selected message into one or more target conversations.
Set `forwardConversationIdsJson` to a JSON array of route-safe ids such as `["group:{{groupId}}"]` or `["dm:{{userId}}"]`.

Permanent delete for moderation/admin tooling only.
Normal chat UX should use `Recall Message` instead.
A non-admin token should receive `403` here.

exec: pm.test('admin delete succeeded or non-admin was correctly rejected', function () {

request.description:
Lists avatar files uploaded by the current user.
The test script stores `currentAvatarKey` from the `isInUse` item and stores a reusable `avatarLibraryKey` from the first non-in-use item when available.

Deletes an avatar file from the upload library.
This succeeds only when the selected file is not the current avatar in use.

Controls: `uploadTarget`, `uploadEntityId`, `uploadFileName`, `uploadContentType`, `uploadFileSize`.
Auto-resolves `entityId` from `currentUserId` / `conversationId` / `reportId` when `uploadEntityId` is blank.
On success, Postman stores `uploadKey` and the target-specific key variable (`avatarKey`, `messageAttachmentKey`, or `reportMediaKey`).

Reads `resolvedUploadKey` from `uploadKey` by default.
If `uploadKey` is blank, Postman falls back to `avatarKey`, `messageAttachmentKey`, or `reportMediaKey` based on `uploadTarget`.
The signed URL is saved into `presignedDownloadUrl`.

Multipart upload helper for quick manual testing.
Auto-resolves `entityId` from `currentUserId` / `conversationId` / `reportId` when `uploadEntityId` is blank.
On success, Postman stores `uploadKey`, the target-specific key variable, and `uploadedAssetUrl`.

Deletes the currently selected upload key for the chosen target.
When `uploadKey` is blank, Postman falls back to the target-specific key variable.
Successful deletion clears the matching stored key from the environment.
